_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Column-docs baseline scrub — add doc= to every Column in orch/db/models.py, delete the baseline file, and flip check-column-docs from warn-first to blocking

steps.description: Wave 1 scrub: add a one-line doc= argument to every Column declaration in WorkItem (33) + StepRun (28) + ProjectDoc (21) + BatchItem (21) = 103 columns. Source descriptions from docs/IW_AI_Core_Database_Schema.md where present; infer from column name/type/usage otherwise. Edit only orch/db/models.py. Do NOT regenerate or delete the baseline file (S04 owns that). Do NOT touch Makefile, GH workflow, strategy doc, tracker, schema doc, or any migration file. Record wave_scrub_count=103 and remaining_baseline_count in the report.

Wave 2 scrub: add doc= to every Column in WorkflowStep (20) + DocGenerationJob (19) + CodeIndexJob (18) + TestRun (17) + Batch (16) = 90 columns. Same sourcing rule + same prohibitions as S01. Verify S01 reported complete before starting. Record wave_scrub_count=90 and cumulative_scrub_count=193.

Wave 3 scrub: add doc= to every Column in the ten OSS/chat/runtime classes — OssFinding (15) + DocIndexJob (15) + ProjectOssJob (13) + PendingMigrationLog (13) + FixCycle (12) + OssScan (11) + ChatTab (11) + ChatSummarizationJob (11) + ChatConversation (11) + AgentRuntimeOption (11) = 123 columns. Same rules as S01/S02. Verify S01 and S02 reported complete. Record wave_scrub_count=123 and cumulative_scrub_count=316.

Wave 4 closing wave — five distinct actions in this order: (1) scrub all 22 remaining model classes (~134 columns); (2) regenerate baseline against /dev/null and confirm zero violations, then `git rm orch/db/column_docs_baseline.txt`; (3) flip Makefile `quality` target from `@$(MAKE) check-column-docs || true` to `@$(MAKE) check-column-docs` AND update the `check-column-docs:` target recipe to drop the now-deleted `--baseline orch/db/column_docs_baseline.txt` argument (run the scanner in pure-audit mode; leaving the stale path crashes it with FileNotFoundError) AND flip `.github/workflows/test-quality.yml` line `- run: make check-column-docs || true` to `- run: make check-column-docs`; (4) update `docs/IW_AI_Core_Testing_Strategy.md` §5 row + `ai-dev/work/TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md` §8 row 4.5.followup → ✅(CR-00092, 2026-05-28, blocking) + §11 new top changelog entry + header v1.8 → v1.9; (5) MANDATORY AC8 deliberate-break-then-revert demonstration recorded in the report (pick one column, remove its doc=, confirm `make check-column-docs` exits non-zero with that column named, restore, confirm exit 0, confirm `git diff` empty). DaemonEvent caveat: `doc=` lives on the Column declaration regardless of the python `event_metadata` alias. Forbidden: editing docs/IW_AI_Core_Database_Schema.md, any orch/db/migrations/versions/** file, any tests/** file (apart from auto-format).

Per-agent review of S01-S04. CRITICAL bars: scanner against /dev/null exits 0; baseline file absent on disk AND from git ls-files; `|| true` is gone from BOTH Makefile and GH workflow; `make quality` exits 0 on the clean tree AND non-zero with synthetic regression; scope diff = exactly the 6 Impacted Paths (no docs/IW_AI_Core_Database_Schema.md, no orch/db/migrations/versions/**, no tests/** apart from auto-format); strategy doc §5 + tracker §8/§11 + tracker header all consistent on CR-00092 + 2026-05-28; wave counts (S01=103, S02=90, S03=123, S04≈134, cumulative=450) consistent across reports; AC8 demonstration recorded in S04 report with specific column name and both exit codes; `doc=` quality spot-check across ≥10 columns. DaemonEvent.event_metadata: doc= lives on the Column() declaration, not the python attribute alias.

Global cross-step review executing AC1–AC8 mechanically. AC1: scanner --baseline /dev/null exits 0. AC2: baseline file absent + not in git ls-files. AC3: synthetic-regression test of make quality (remove one doc=, confirm non-zero, restore, confirm 0, confirm git diff empty). AC4: grep check-column-docs in GH workflow shows no `|| true`. AC5: make quality clean exits 0. AC6: strategy doc §5 row + tracker §8 row 4.5.followup + tracker §11 new entry + header v1.8 → v1.9 all consistent. AC7: directional scope diff = exactly the 6 Impacted Paths (no schema doc, no migrations, no unrelated files). AC8: S04 report's deliberate-break section names a specific column with both invocation exit codes. Cross-step numeric anchors: 103+90+123+S04.wave_scrub_count=450; cumulative_scrub_count=450; remaining_baseline_count=0; baseline_deleted=true. Run `make test-unit` AND `uv run pytest tests/orch/db/test_column_docs.py -v`. Compare time/fix-cycle footprint vs CR-00081 (78-entry assertion scrub) and CR-00085 (scanner kit).
QV: Assertion scanner (no new test files — should be a no-op delta)
QV: Type checking (must catch any malformed mapped_column(...) added by the scrub)
QV: Unit tests (tests/orch/db/test_column_docs.py runs here — covers the scanner against the live tree)

QV: Diff coverage — most of this CR's diff is metadata-only doc= additions which are not executable; coverage should be unaffected (note: orch/db/models.py is class-level code executed at import time, so coverage stays high)
QV: Secret scanning (gitleaks) — no secrets expected; metadata + config change only

Self-assessment via the iw-item-analyze skill. Anchor on: (a) did the four-wave split sizes (90/103/123/134) hold up or did any wave run out of context; (b) was the DaemonEvent.event_metadata rename trap handled correctly (doc= on Column declaration, not on attribute alias); (c) inferred-vs-sourced description fraction (high inferred fraction = schema-doc rot signal for a future CR); (d) scope discipline (was docs/IW_AI_Core_Database_Schema.md or any migration file accidentally touched); (e) wave-count math sums to 450; (f) S04 did scrub → delete baseline → flip gate in correct order; (g) AC8 deliberate-break demonstration actually run (not hand-waved); (h) time/fix-cycle footprint vs CR-00081 (78-entry analog) and CR-00085 (scanner kit precedent); (i) every wave's tdd_red_evidence used the `n/a — content-only ...` form.
